cards.back: IEEE 802.15.4g is a wireless personal area network (WPAN) standard
IEEE 802.15.4e is an extension of the 802.15.4 standard, supporting higher data rates
IEEE 1901.2a is a wireless power transmission standard, part of the IEEE 1901 family

cards.front: What is the main difference between LoRaWAN and NB-IoT?
cards.back: LoRaWAN uses spread spectrum and is more suitable for long-range communication, while NB-IoT uses traditional cellular technology and is better suited for short-range communication
IEEE 802.11ah is a low-power wide-area network (LPWAN) standard, a variant of the 802.11 family of Wi-Fi standards
